generatedScreen.title: Tramo - Landing Page Operativa
generatedScreen.prompt:
Design a long-form B2B landing page for Tramo. Use {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_1}} as the logo.

SECTION 1: HERO
- LEFT: Badge 'Para gestoras con 10+ alojamientos'. H1 'Convierte la energía de tu cartera turística en margen operativo.' Subcopy mentioning real-time consumption, bookings, CUPS/Datadis. CTAs: 'Diagnosticar mi cartera' (orange) and 'Ver dashboard demo' (outline).
- RIGHT: Interactive Dashboard Mock (HTML/CSS). Title 'Panel de control · 32 alojamientos monitorizados'. Metric '18.4 kW ahora' with green pulse. ALERT CARD '⚠ VGO-014 · vacío desde 11:00 · 4.2 kWh fuera de reserva · 42 EUR estimados'. Timeline, P1/P2 (Actual 4.6 kW vs Recomendado 3.3 kW), OMIE sparkline, battery status, 'Informe Abril listo'. Interaction: hover on alert card makes metric glow orange.

SECTION 2: PAIN TRIPTYCH (3 Cards)
- Card 1: 'Consumo fantasma'. Before: factura llega 2 meses tarde. After: Tramo avisa en minutos (VGO-014 vacío). Evidence: timestamps, kWh, EUR.
- Card 2: 'Tarifa y potencia heredadas'. Before: CUPS sin revisar. After: P2 recomendado 3.3 kW, ahorro 180€/año. Evidence: P1/P2 comparison.
- Card 3: 'Propietarios sin explicación'. Before: propietario ve coste sin evidencia. After: Informe mensual (412 kWh, 3 acciones). Evidence: report KPIs.

SECTION 3: SYSTEM FLOW
A diagram-style section showing [PMS / Reservas] + [Sensores / consumo real] + [CUPS / Datadis / facturas] + [Tarifas / OMIE] flowing (↓) into 'Tramo atribuye cada kWh' then (↓) to [Alertas operativas] → [Decisiones] → [Informes propietarios]. Use clean icons and arrows.

SECTION 4: CORE MODULES (4 cards with visual evidence and metric cards)
- 1. CONSUMO EN TIEMPO REAL: Gauge + alert queue. Evidence 'VGO-014 · 4.2 kWh sin reserva'.
- 2. TARIFA Y POTENCIA POR CUPS: P1/P2 bars. Evidence 'P2 actual 4.6 kW -> recomendado 3.3 kW'.
- 3. REGLAS OPERATIVAS POR RESERVA: Timeline with appliance icons. Evidence 'Regla: apagar AC 30 min post-checkout'.
- 4. INFORMES A PROPIETARIOS: Report preview. Evidence '412 kWh · 3 acciones · 42.50 EUR'.

SECTION 5: PRODUCT PROOF DASHBOARD
Property list (VGO-014, COR-007, etc.), Consumption chart (occupied green vs idle gray vs anomaly orange), Alert queue (4 active), Tariff recommendation card. Date: 'Mayo 2026'.

SECTION 6: TRUST / PILOT SAFETY
Explain 'Datos que usamos' (PMS, CUPS, etc.) and 'Datos que NO pedimos' (Credenciales). Highlight 'Piloto seguro': diagnose first, no installation needed yet.

SECTION 7: LEAD FORM
Title 'Solicita un diagnóstico de cartera'. Fields: Nombre, Empresa, Email, Nº alojamientos, Ciudad/Región, PMS, Datadis Access (Yes/No), Baterías (Yes/No), Dolor principal, Módulos, Mensaje. CTA 'Solicitar diagnóstico'. Privacy text: no credentials via form.

FOOTER: Wordmark, © 2026 Tramo, Links.

Style: Background #fcf9f8, Cards #ffffff with #ead8cd border, Accent orange #e6813a. Fonts: Plus Jakarta Sans (Headings), Inter (Body), JetBrains Mono (Metrics). Technical feel with grid lines and ticks. No eco/green cliches. Spanish language.
## Available Images
You MUST include the following images in this design. You must reference them
by putting their exact placeholder IDs in `src` attributes or CSS
`background-image` rules. Do NOT use external URLs or generate new
placeholders. Preserve these IDs exactly as written. Read each image's
Description to understand its subject, intended section, and visual role.

- {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_1}}
  Alt Text: Tramo Technical Logo
  Description: A monoline segmented bracket/path logo with three interval segments, one signal dot in orange (#e6813a), and small tick marks. It has a technical, energy infrastructure diagram feel with no eco/leaf/bolt cliches.. Brand logo
  Dimensions: 512x512
  Aspect Ratio: 1.00
  Colors: #1c1b1b, #e6813a
generatedScreen.name: projects/5322604060056266365/screens/96f651bc65804e168c72b182041558c1

theme.designMd:
---
name: Tramo Infrastructure
colors:
  surface: '#fff8f5'
  surface-dim: '#e8d7ce'
  surface-bright: '#fff8f5'
  surface-container-lowest: '#ffffff'
  surface-container-low: '#fff1ea'

  surface-container-high: '#f7e5db'
  surface-container-highest: '#f1dfd6'

  on-surface-variant: '#554338'
  inverse-surface: '#392e28'
  inverse-on-surface: '#ffede5'
  outline: '#887367'
  outline-variant: '#dbc1b3'
  surface-tint: '#984700'
  primary: '#984700'
  on-primary: '#ffffff'
  primary-container: '#e6813a'
  on-primary-container: '#562500'
  inverse-primary: '#ffb68a'
  secondary: '#984700'
  on-secondary: '#ffffff'
  secondary-container: '#fe9650'
  on-secondary-container: '#6f3200'
  tertiary: '#00677f'
  on-tertiary: '#ffffff'
  tertiary-container: '#00a9ce'
  on-tertiary-container: '#003846'
  error: '#ba1a1a'
  on-error: '#ffffff'
  error-container: '#ffdad6'
  on-error-container: '#93000a'
  primary-fixed: '#ffdbc8'
  primary-fixed-dim: '#ffb68a'
  on-primary-fixed: '#321300'
  on-primary-fixed-variant: '#743500'
  secondary-fixed: '#ffdbc8'
  secondary-fixed-dim: '#ffb68a'
  on-secondary-fixed: '#321300'
  on-secondary-fixed-variant: '#743500'
  tertiary-fixed: '#b6ebff'
  tertiary-fixed-dim: '#57d5fc'
  on-tertiary-fixed: '#001f28'
  on-tertiary-fixed-variant: '#004e60'
  background: '#fff8f5'
  on-background: '#231a14'
  surface-variant: '#f1dfd6'
typography:
  display-lg:
    fontFamily: Plus Jakarta Sans
    fontSize: 48px
    fontWeight: '600'
    lineHeight: 56px
    letterSpacing: -0.02em
  headline-lg:
    fontFamily: Plus Jakarta Sans
    fontSize: 32px
    fontWeight: '600'
    lineHeight: 40px
    letterSpacing: -0.01em
  headline-md:
    fontFamily: Plus Jakarta Sans
    fontSize: 24px
    fontWeight: '600'
    lineHeight: 32px
  body-lg:
    fontFamily: Inter
    fontSize: 18px
    fontWeight: '400'
    lineHeight: 28px
  body-md:
    fontFamily: Inter
    fontSize: 16px
    fontWeight: '400'
    lineHeight: 24px
  body-sm:
    fontFamily: Inter
    fontSize: 14px
    fontWeight: '400'
    lineHeight: 20px
  data-lg:
    fontFamily: JetBrains Mono
    fontSize: 20px
    fontWeight: '500'
    lineHeight: 24px
  data-md:
    fontFamily: JetBrains Mono
    fontSize: 14px
    fontWeight: '500'
    lineHeight: 20px
  label-caps:
    fontFamily: JetBrains Mono
    fontSize: 12px
    fontWeight: '600'
    lineHeight: 16px
    letterSpacing: 0.05em
spacing:
  unit: 4px
  gutter: 24px
  margin: 32px
  container-max: 1440px
---

## Brand & Style

This design system is built for the high-stakes environment of B2B energy operations, where technical precision meets editorial clarity. The aesthetic is inspired by modern industrial documentation and high-end architectural journals—balancing a clean, "paper-like" tactile quality with the urgent, live-data needs of energy infrastructure.

The visual direction leans into a refined **Minimalism** enriched with **Industrial Utility**. It utilizes a sophisticated "ink-on-paper" contrast to ensure legibility during long operational shifts. The interface avoids unnecessary decorative flourishes, opting instead for structural elements like grid lines, technical ticks, and monospaced data readouts to evoke a sense of controlled, real-time monitoring.

**Target Audience:** Energy grid operators, logistics managers, and technical analysts.
**Emotional Response:** Composed, authoritative, precise, and reliable.

## Colors

The palette is anchored by a warm, organic base that reduces eye strain compared to pure digital whites. 

- **Foundation:** The "Cream/Paper" background (`#fcf9f8`) provides a sophisticated, low-glare canvas that feels more like a physical dashboard than a software screen.
- **Accents:** "Signal Orange" (`#e6813a`) is used sparingly for interactive triggers and critical status indicators. It represents the "live" energy flowing through the system.
- **Typography:** We use "Ink" (`#1c1b1b`) for primary headings to maintain maximum contrast, while "Muted Brown" (`#554338`) handles secondary metadata and labels, grounding the UI in an earthy, industrial tone.
- **Functional:** Success and Error states use highly legible, professional tones (Teal Green and Deep Red) that harmonize with the warm background without appearing jarring.

## Typography

Typography is used to distinguish between narrative content and technical data.

- **Headings:** Plus Jakarta Sans provides a contemporary, clean geometric structure. Use it for page titles and section headers to establish a clear editorial hierarchy.
- **Body:** Inter is the workhorse for all long-form text and interface controls, chosen for its exceptional readability at small sizes.
- **Data & Metrics:** JetBrains Mono is utilized for all numerical values, timestamps, and technical IDs. The monospaced nature ensures that columns of numbers align perfectly in live-updating tables, reinforcing the "instrumentation" feel.

## Layout & Spacing

This design system employs a **Fixed Grid** philosophy for desktop layouts to maintain the structured feel of a technical manual, while transitioning to a **Fluid Grid** for mobile devices.

- **Grid:** A 12-column system is used for dashboard views. Components should align to a strict 4px baseline grid to ensure vertical rhythm.
- **Whitespace:** Generous padding is a core principle. This "negative space" is intentional; it prevents cognitive overload in data-heavy environments.
- **Infrastructure Accents:** Sections are often separated by 1px solid borders (`#ead8cd`) rather than shadows. Small "tick" marks or hair-thin grid lines may be used in the background of charts or card headers to emphasize the technical nature of the platform.

## Elevation & Depth

To maintain the "Sourceful-inspired" editorial aesthetic, this design system avoids heavy shadows. Depth is communicated through **Tonal Layering** and **Crisp Outlines**.

- **Surfaces:** The background is the lowest layer (`#fcf9f8`). Interactive cards and containers sit "on top" of this layer using a pure white (`#ffffff`) fill.
- **Borders:** Every card and significant UI container must have a 1px solid border using the `#ead8cd` token. This provides a clear physical boundary without the "fuzziness" of shadows.
- **Active States:** Instead of raising an element on the Z-axis, active or focused states are indicated by a 2px "Signal Orange" border or a subtle outer glow (`0px 0px 8px rgba(230, 129, 58, 0.2)`).

## Shapes

The shape language is strictly **Sharp**. 

Rounded corners are avoided to maintain an architectural and engineering-focused aesthetic. 90-degree angles suggest stability, precision, and efficiency. This applies to buttons, input fields, cards, and dropdown menus. 

Small exceptions can be made for "Status Pips" (circular indicators for live connectivity) or specific iconography, but all structural containers must remain square-edged.

## Components

### Buttons
Buttons are rectangular with sharp 0px corners.
- **Primary:** Background `#e6813a`, Text `#ffffff`. On hover, darken to `#984700`.
- **Secondary:** Transparent background, 1px border `#1c1b1b`, Text `#1c1b1b`.
- **Ghost:** No background or border. Uses `label-caps` typography.

### Cards
White background (`#ffffff`) with a 1px solid border (`#ead8cd`). Headers within cards should be separated by a horizontal 1px line, with the title in `headline-md` and metadata in `data-md`.

### Input Fields
Strictly rectangular. Use a 1px border of `#ead8cd`. Labels should use `label-caps` typography and sit above the field. Focused fields transition their border color to `#e6813a`.

### Status Chips
Used for operational states.
- **Success:** `#0f766e` background with white text.
- **Alert:** `#ba1a1a` background with white text.
- **Neutral:** `#554338` background with white text.
All chips use `label-caps` at a reduced size (10px or 11px) for a "tag" effect.

### Infrastructure Ticks
Used on the edges of cards or along the axes of data visualizations. These are 1px wide, 4px long lines in `#ead8cd` that serve as decorative but functional-looking visual anchors.
